```json
    {"name": "FreqtradeBot", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "why": "2584 lines containing the core trading loop (process, enter_positions, execute_entry, handle_trade, execute_trade_exit). Methods like enter_positions have 7-level-deep nested loops (O(n^7) risk) and 73 callers."},
    {"name": "Telegram", "file": "freqtrade/rpc/telegram.py", "why": "2144-line class that centralizes all Telegram bot command handling, message formatting, and conversation flows in one file."},

    {"name": "IFreqaiModel", "file": "freqtrade/freqai/freqai_interface.py", "why": "1009-line AI/ML model interface juggling training, backtesting, inference, data caching, and live prediction pipelines in one class."},
    {"name": "FreqaiDataKitchen", "file": "freqtrade/freqai/data_kitchen.py", "why": "1004-line data preparation class handling feature engineering, label creation, train/test splitting, normalization, PCA, and data persistence."},
    {"name": "Trade (SQLAlchemy)", "file": "freqtrade/persistence/trade_model.py", "why": "485-line SQLAlchemy model with 96-caller commit(), 73-caller get_trades(), plus query and migration logic embedded in the same class."}
  ],
  "methodology": "Used the code graph intelligence agent with tools: get_architecture (hotspot analysis, god component detection), analyze_hotspots (fan-in, complexity, risk levels), smart_review (file-level size, complexity, coupling, nesting-depth analysis), explain_symbol (per-symbol cyclomatic/cognitive complexity), and query_graph (structural queries). All measurements are structural — no individual file was exhaustively read. File line counts are class sizes from the hotspot/god-component analysis; function complexities are cyclomatic complexity from the code graph's call-graph analysis.",
  "summary": "Freqtrade shows major scalability concerns. The Exchange class (4036 lines), FreqtradeBot (2584 lines), and Telegram (2144 lines) are critical god objects that violate single-responsibility principles. The most complex function (Exchange.get_markets, cyclomatic 24) and the tightest-coupled function (Trade.commit, fan-in 96) are both in these monoliths. Several methods have extreme nesting depths (Backtesting.backtest: 5 levels, FreqtradeBot.enter_positions: 7 levels, LocalTrade.select_order: 6 levels) indicating O(n^5+) algorithmic complexity risk. Key refactoring targets are: splitting Exchange by domain (markets, orders, OHLCV, leverage), extracting FreqtradeBot's entry/exit handling into separate services, and breaking the persistence layer to separate ORM from business logic."
}
```

```json
  "summary": "Freqtrade is a mature, large codebase (~200K+ lines of Python) with significant scalability concerns. The exchange abstraction layer (Exchange class at 4100+ lines) and the central bot controller (FreqtradeBot at 2700+ lines) are the most severe bottlenecks, acting as god objects that violate the Single Responsibility Principle. The `freqtrade/exchange/exchange.py` file alone handles ~100 distinct responsibilities ranging from exchange connectivity to funding fee calculations. Several functions are marked with noqa: C901 (high cyclomatic complexity). The persistence layer's Trade model (2136 lines) also exhibits excessive coupling. Positive aspects include a well-structured plugin system (pairlists, protections) that follows composition-over-inheritance, and a clean separation of FreqAI models. Recommendation: decompose Exchange into domain-specific services (MarketDataService, OrderService, FeeService, AccountService); split FreqtradeBot into focused handlers (EntryHandler, ExitHandler, RiskManager); reduce IStrategy interface surface area; and extract the Trade model's calculation methods into strategy-pattern classes."
```
